game_name: World of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in platforming paths
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring avoidance or cape-based attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Playing cards displayed on-screen; turned face-down upon damage
danger_signals.meaning: Player damage state; loss of cards indicates proximity to failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprites at level conclusion
danger_signals.meaning: Significant challenge requiring defeat to progress and unlock new magic spells

danger_signals.visual_form: Level-specific obstacles (underwater pressure, cloud instability, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-dependent threats requiring mastery of new magical abilities

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemies convert into doves, cards, or harmless objects
safety_signals.meaning: Successful neutralization; threat eliminated and rendered benign

safety_signals.visual_form: Characters standing on each other's shoulders in two-player mode
safety_signals.meaning: Safe collaborative mechanic enabling access to otherwise blocked areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Character animation of cape-based offensive action
safety_signals.meaning: Active player agency; ability to convert danger into safety

safety_signals.visual_form: New magical ability granted after boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Progression unlock; increased capability for traversing subsequent challenges

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ability to squeeze through gaps; exclusive alternate routes in single-player mode
Larger build; requires alternative pathways Mickey can navigate; different realm access in single-player

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magical box ownership; source of world-displacement plot
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence implied through magical world creation; not directly encountered until implied final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest environment with Disney Silly Symphonies aesthetic influences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard platforming with basic enemy encounters and environmental navigation
Introduction of flight mechanics via magic carpet spell; altered gravity and movement physics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aquatic environment with water-based visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bubble-based traversal mechanic; different enemy types and movement constraints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior architectural space with literary/knowledge setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Puzzle-solving emphasis; platforming adapted to indoor spatial constraints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Playing-card soldiers from Alice in Wonderland; castle-garden section with Disney film references
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Convergence of all previous mechanics; final challenge territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller character sprite enabling gap navigation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to exclusive sub-stages and alternative realms unavailable in Donald path

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger character sprite requiring circumnavigation of tight spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Divergent platforming pathways; entirely different realm accessibility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both Mickey and Donald visible simultaneously on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shoulder-stacking interactions, rope mechanics, and gap-pulling assistance; shared health pool

environmental_storytelling.example: Music, artwork, and animation directly echo Snow White, Pinocchio, Fantasia, Sleeping Beauty, The Sword in the Stone, and The Little Mermaid, establishing world tonality through artistic consistency
Each of five principal stages (Enchanted Forest, Among the Clouds, Underwater Adventure, The Library, The Magic Box) communicates distinct magical realm identity through environment design
Boss defeats unlock new magic spells, visually demonstrating narrative advancement and capability expansion
Divergent pathways and realm access based on Mickey vs. Donald selection communicates individual character agency within shared magical world
Two-player shoulder-standing and assistance mechanics visually communicate character interdependence within magical context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Cape flourishing attack; graceful navigation; gap-traversal capability
Cape flourishing attack; alternative route-finding; larger collision model requiring circumnavigation
Transformation into harmless objects (doves, cards, etc.); threat neutralization
Yield new magic spell; unlock progression to subsequent stage
Shoulder-stacking positioning; rope-based assistance; mutual gap-pulling mechanics
Cards displayed face-up on screen UI
Cards turned face-down; visual indicator of player vulnerability

notes: World of Illusion employs Disney intellectual property and aesthetic consistency to establish world-state communication. The game communicates danger primarily through enemy placement, health-card visualization, and stage-specific hazards. Safety is communicated through enemy transformation, cooperative mechanics, and spell-acquisition progression. Faction identity is minimal; distinction centers on character-specific physical capabilities (gap-squeezing vs. alternative routing) rather than opposed political entities. The magical-box premise establishes all stages as alternate realities, with stage theming (forest, clouds, underwater, library, magic box) serving as primary world-state markers. Character selection drives significant environmental storytelling divergence, suggesting individual agency within collective magical displacement narrative.
